annotations.label: Cyclic group (lead)

anchor.snippet: a cyclic group or monogenous group is a group

annotations.note: `IsCyclic` is defined as the predicate that there exists a generator under `Pow G ℤ`.

annotations.label: Classification of cyclic groups up to isomorphism

anchor.snippet: Every infinite cyclic group is isomorphic to the additive group

annotations.note: Mathlib provides `intCyclicAddEquiv : ℤ ≃+ G` for infinite cyclic `G` and `zmodAddCyclicAddEquiv : ZMod (Nat.card G) ≃+ G` for finite cyclic `G`.

annotations.label: Cyclic groups are abelian; finitely generated abelian groups are products of cyclic groups

anchor.snippet: Every cyclic group is an abelian group

annotations.note: Commutativity is `IsCyclic.commGroup`/`IsCyclic.isMulCommutative`; the structure theorem is `AddCommGroup.equiv_free_prod_directSum_zmod`.

annotations.label: Cyclic groups of prime order are simple

anchor.snippet: Every cyclic group of prime order is a simple group

annotations.note: A group of prime order is simple via `isSimpleGroup_of_prime_card` (and `Group.is_simple_iff_prime_card`).

annotations.label: Cyclic subgroup generated by g and order of an element

anchor.snippet: called the cyclic subgroup generated by

annotations.note: Cyclic subgroup generated by `g` is `Subgroup.zpowers g`; the order is `orderOf g` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclic group

anchor.snippet: A cyclic group is a group which is equal to one of its cyclic subgroups

annotations.note: `IsCyclic G` formalizes the property of being generated by a single element.

annotations.label: Standard finite cyclic group C_n

anchor.snippet: For a finite cyclic group

annotations.note: The standard cyclic group of order `n` is realized as `ZMod n` with `ZMod.instIsAddCyclic`.

annotations.label: Complex 6th roots of unity

anchor.snippet: the set of complex 6th roots of unity

annotations.note: Mathlib has the general `rootsOfUnity n R` and `Complex.isPrimitiveRoot_exp`, but no specific example fixed at n = 6.

anchors.snippet: the set of complex 6th roots of unity

annotations.label: Infinite cyclic group

anchor.snippet: in an infinite cyclic group

annotations.note: Infinite cyclic groups are `IsCyclic G` with `[Infinite G]`; `intCyclicMulEquiv` provides the isomorphism to `Multiplicative ℤ`.

annotations.label: Monogenous group (Bourbaki)

anchor.snippet: Bourbaki introduced the term monogenous group

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `IsCyclic` for the same concept, with no separate `monogenous` terminology.

annotations.label: Integers under addition

anchor.snippet: The set of integers

annotations.note: `IsAddCyclic ℤ` is registered as an instance with `1` as generator.

annotations.label: Integers modulo n under addition

anchor.snippet: the set of integers modulo

annotations.note: `ZMod.instIsAddCyclic` provides the cyclic instance for `ZMod n`.

annotations.label: Generators of Z/nZ are units coprime to n

anchor.snippet: is a generator of this group if

annotations.note: `ZMod.isUnit_iff_coprime` characterizes the units of `ZMod n` (= generators of the additive group) as elements coprime to n.

annotations.label: Z/pZ as finite field F_p

anchor.snippet: is a finite field

annotations.note: The field structure on `ZMod p` for prime `p` is given by `ZMod.instField`.

annotations.label: When (Z/nZ)^× is cyclic

anchor.snippet: but is so whenever

annotations.note: `ZMod.isCyclic_units_iff` gives the full characterization of when `(ZMod n)ˣ` is cyclic.

annotations.label: Primitive root modulo n

anchor.snippet: its generators are called primitive roots modulo

annotations.note: Mathlib has the general `IsPrimitiveRoot` predicate but no dedicated `primitiveRootMod` definition for elements of `(ZMod n)ˣ`.

annotations.label: Finite subgroup of multiplicative group of a field is cyclic

anchor.snippet: every finite subgroup of the multiplicative group of any field is cyclic

annotations.note: `isCyclic_subgroup_units` proves any finite subgroup of `Rˣ` is cyclic over an integral domain, generalizing the field case.

annotations.label: Rotational symmetries of a polygon

anchor.snippet: The set of rotational symmetries of a polygon

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the rotational-symmetry group of a regular polygon as a named example.

annotations.label: Circle group is not cyclic

anchor.snippet: is not cyclic, because there is no single rotation

annotations.note: No statement asserting `¬ IsCyclic Circle` appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n-th root of unity

anchor.snippet: is a complex number whose

annotations.note: `rootsOfUnity n R` is the subgroup of `Rˣ` of elements `ζ` with `ζ ^ n = 1`.

annotations.label: n-th roots of unity form a cyclic group

anchor.snippet: The set of all

annotations.note: `rootsOfUnity.isCyclic` is the instance making `rootsOfUnity n R` cyclic in any integral domain.

annotations.label: Cyclic extension

anchor.snippet: A field extension is called a cyclic extension

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses cyclic extensions as `IsCyclic Gal(L/K)` (e.g. in `KummerExtension.lean`) without a separate `IsCyclicExtension` predicate.

annotations.label: Galois group of finite field extension is cyclic

anchor.snippet: its Galois group is always finite and cyclic

annotations.note: An anonymous instance `IsCyclic Gal(L/K)` for finite `L` is given in `Mathlib/FieldTheory/Finite/Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Realization of finite cyclic groups as Galois groups over finite fields

anchor.snippet: Conversely, given a finite field

annotations.note: The example `IsCyclic Gal(Extension k p n / k)` realizes any finite cyclic group as a Galois group over a finite field.

annotations.label: Subgroups and quotients of cyclic groups are cyclic

anchor.snippet: All subgroups and quotient groups of cyclic groups are cyclic

annotations.note: `Subgroup.isCyclic` gives subgroups; `isCyclic_of_surjective` covers quotients via the quotient map.

annotations.label: Cyclic group is simple iff its order is prime

anchor.snippet: a cyclic group is simple if and only if its order is prime

annotations.note: `CommGroup.is_simple_iff_prime_card` proves a commutative (hence cyclic-allowing) group is simple iff its order is prime.

annotations.label: Subgroups of Z/nZ correspond to divisors of n

anchor.snippet: the quotient group

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Subgroup.le_zpowers_iff` describing subgroups of a cyclic group as `zpowers (g^n)`, but no explicit bijection between subgroups of `ZMod n` and divisors of `n`.

annotations.label: Every cyclic group is abelian

anchor.snippet: Every cyclic group is abelian

annotations.note: The instance `IsCyclic.isMulCommutative` makes every cyclic group commutative.

annotations.label: Primary cyclic group

anchor.snippet: is called a primary cyclic group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `PrimaryCyclic` definition; prime-power cyclic groups are handled ad hoc via `ZMod (p^k)`.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of finitely generated abelian groups

anchor.snippet: The fundamental theorem of abelian groups states

annotations.note: `AddCommGroup.equiv_free_prod_directSum_zmod` is the structure theorem for finitely generated abelian groups.

annotations.label: Conjugacy classes in a cyclic group

anchor.snippet: each of its conjugacy classes consists of a single element

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes that cyclic groups are commutative, so conjugacy classes are singletons follows, but no statement is explicitly named for cyclic groups' conjugacy classes.

annotations.label: Number of elements of given order in Z/nZ

anchor.snippet: then the number of elements in

annotations.note: `IsCyclic.card_orderOf_eq_totient` shows there are `φ(d)` elements of order `d` in a finite cyclic group whenever `d ∣ Nat.card`.

annotations.label: Characterization of cyclic finite groups by elements of order dividing n

anchor.snippet: is a finite group in which

annotations.note: `isCyclic_of_card_pow_eq_one_le` proves a finite group is cyclic if at most `n` elements satisfy `x^n = 1` for every `n`.

annotations.label: Order of element m in Z/nZ

anchor.snippet: The order of an element

annotations.note: `ZMod.addOrderOf_coe` proves `addOrderOf (m : ZMod n) = n / Nat.gcd n m`.

annotations.label: Chinese remainder theorem for cyclic groups

anchor.snippet: then the direct product of two cyclic groups

annotations.note: `ZMod.chineseRemainder` provides the ring (and group) isomorphism `ZMod (m*n) ≃+* ZMod m × ZMod n` for coprime `m, n`; `Group.isCyclic_prod_iff` also captures the cyclic product characterization.

annotations.label: Groups of prime order are cyclic

anchor.snippet: then any group with

annotations.note: `isCyclic_of_prime_card` proves any group of prime cardinality is cyclic.

annotations.label: Cyclic number

anchor.snippet: is called a cyclic number

annotations.note: No `Nat.IsCyclicNumber` or analogous definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group presentation of cyclic groups

anchor.snippet: cyclic groups have group presentation

annotations.note: Mathlib has `PresentedGroup` but no instance computing the presentation `⟨x | xⁿ⟩` for cyclic groups.

annotations.label: Endomorphism ring of Z/nZ

anchor.snippet: The endomorphism ring of the abelian group

annotations.note: No direct `AddMonoid.End (ZMod n) ≃+* ZMod n`; relatedly, `ZMod.AddAutEquivUnits` and `addMonoidEndRingEquivInt` provide the unit-group and endomorphism-ring infrastructure.

annotations.label: Automorphism group of Z/nZ

anchor.snippet: so the automorphism group of

annotations.note: `ZMod.AddAutEquivUnits : AddAut (ZMod n) ≃* (ZMod n)ˣ` gives the automorphism group description.

annotations.label: Endomorphism ring of Z

anchor.snippet: the endomorphism ring of the additive group

annotations.note: `addMonoidEndRingEquivInt` gives `AddMonoid.End A ≃+* Module.End ℤ A`; specialized to `A = ℤ` combined with `RingEquiv.moduleEndSelf` gives `End ℤ ≃+* ℤ` but no single direct decl.

annotations.label: Tensor product of cyclic groups

anchor.section: Tensor product and Hom of cyclic groups
anchor.snippet: The tensor product

annotations.note: No specific `ZMod m ⊗ ZMod n ≃ ZMod (gcd m n)` statement was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hom group of cyclic groups

anchor.section: Tensor product and Hom of cyclic groups
anchor.snippet: For the Hom group

annotations.note: No formalization of `Hom(ZMod m, ZMod n) ≃ ZMod (gcd m n)` was found.

annotations.label: Virtually cyclic group

anchor.snippet: A group is called virtually cyclic

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `VirtuallyCyclic` definition.

annotations.label: Characterization of infinite virtually cyclic groups by ends

anchor.snippet: An infinite group is virtually cyclic if and only if

annotations.note: Neither virtually cyclic groups nor ends of groups appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abelian subgroups of hyperbolic groups are virtually cyclic

anchor.snippet: Every abelian subgroup of a Gromov hyperbolic group is virtually cyclic

annotations.note: Neither Gromov-hyperbolic groups nor virtually cyclic groups are in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Procyclic group

anchor.snippet: A profinite group is called procyclic

annotations.note: No `Procyclic` definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Locally cyclic group

anchor.snippet: A locally cyclic group is a group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `LocallyCyclic` definition.

annotations.label: Additive group of the rationals is locally cyclic

anchor.snippet: the additive group of the rational numbers

annotations.note: Without `LocallyCyclic` defined, this example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Locally cyclic iff subgroup lattice is distributive

anchor.snippet: A group is locally cyclic if and only if

annotations.note: Neither locally cyclic groups nor this characterization are in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclically ordered group

anchor.snippet: A cyclically ordered group is a group

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define cyclically ordered groups.

annotations.label: Finite subgroups of cyclically ordered groups are cyclic

anchor.snippet: Every finite subgroup of a cyclically ordered group is cyclic

annotations.note: Without cyclically ordered groups defined, this theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Metacyclic group

anchor.snippet: A metacyclic group is a group containing a cyclic normal subgroup

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `Metacyclic` definition.

annotations.label: Polycyclic group

anchor.snippet: A group is polycyclic if it has a finite descending sequence

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `Polycyclic` definition.

annotations.label: Finitely generated abelian and nilpotent groups are polycyclic

anchor.snippet: Every finitely generated abelian group or nilpotent group is polycyclic

annotations.note: Without polycyclicity defined, this statement is not formalized in Mathlib.
